pages.raw_text:
At the annual meeting of the
stockholders of the Inland Empire
system yesterday, Jay P. Graves,
F. Lewis Clark, F. A. Blackwell, A.
l.. White, Aaron Kuhn, Waldo G.
Paine and Will G. Graves were
elected trustees for the coming
year. Officers chosen were Jay P.
Graves president, F. Lewis Clark
vice president, A. L. White second
vice president, w. G. Davidson sec
rotary and H. B. Ferris treasnrr.


In his annual statment to the
stockholders, President Graves
spoke of the Moscow extension hav-
ing been completed and opened for
traffic yesterday. He said business
was getting better for the company
and that it was coming out Of the
effects of the financial depression
of last fall, which had caused a
suspension of dividends. If the
present rate of increase of business
continued, dividends will again be
paid in 1909 The earning during
the year were $1,139,1S6, as against
operating expenses of $S07 $500
The assets of the company are esti-
mated at $22,673,S010S.


The first regular cars were run
through between Spokane and Mos
COW, Idaho, yesterday on schedule
time. There was business for the
new line from the very first car
The opening of the electric line
into Moscow has been a great event
for that community.
